chunk_summary: Guidelines for managing endurance-limited feeding in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge, focusing on pacing, measurable intake targets, mixed-modality strategies, and escalation to partial tube feeding if needed.
learning_objectives: Understand the characteristics of endurance-limited feeding phenotypes in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Learn staged decision-making steps for optimizing feeding safety and intake targets.
Recognize when to escalate to partial tube feeding and refer to feeding therapy.
Identify reversible medical contributors to feeding endurance limitations.
key_facts: Feeds longer than 30 minutes or persistent sleepiness mid-feed indicate endurance-limited feeding phenotype.
Prolonged feeds increase energy expenditure, delay intake, and elevate aspiration risk, especially in infants with respiratory or cardiac disease.
Daily intake targets should be set in mL/kg/day to ensure adequate nutrition.
Mixed-modality feeding strategies include oral feeds followed by measured top-ups via the safest route.
Reassess feeding plans within 48–72 hours for higher-risk infants to avoid prolonged inadequate intake.
Temporary partial tube feeding should include an explicit weaning plan to avoid oral aversion.
tags_mesh: Feeding and Eating Disorders of Childhood
reference_quote: Prolonged feeds increase energy expenditure, delay intake, and can increase aspiration risk, especially in infants with respiratory or cardiac disease. (Academy of Breastfeeding Medicine Clinical Protocol #12)
document_key_topics: Endurance-limited feeding phenotype in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants
Staged decision-making for feeding safety and intake optimization
Mixed-modality feeding strategies and escalation to tube feeding
Reversible medical contributors to feeding limitations
